question: How can I alleviate performance problems with the octigate database? ITCAM-related question: How can I alleviate performance problems with the octigate database?
documents:
DB2 disk space usage compress top tables octigate database MS Managing Server CAMADALL CAMADINST TECHNOTE (FAQ)

THIS DOCUMENT APPLIES ONLY TO THE FOLLOWING LANGUAGE VERSION(S):
 US English 

QUESTION
 How do I find out the disk space usage of the Managing Server octigate database tables? 

ANSWER
If you have been using the ITCAM Managing Server (MS) for few years to store your monitored Data Collectors (DC), and if you have not used your datatrim script regularly to trim the octigate database tables, then its likely that your tables may have grown over time. So how do you find out which tables are using up the most space? 

Here are the steps to find out: 

1. First connect to the database with the schema id you used to create the tables. In my case, its the default schema db2inst1 shown here:
[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg22003010&aid=1] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg22003010&aid=1]

2. Next do a db2 list tables and you will see 68 tables in the octigate database.

[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg22003010&aid=2] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg22003010&aid=2]

3. If you want to find out which tables have the most number of records, you can do something like this:
select count(*) from <table_name> where you will substitute the <table_name> with the actual table.

For example,

[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg22003010&aid=3] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg22003010&aid=3]

4. Now while this shows the number of records in the table, it doesn't really tell you how big the table is and how much disk space it occupies. This information is stored in the SYSIBMADM.ADMINTABINFO table. You will have to query this table and extract out the disk space from here.

So first find out how the table looks like (its "schema"):
[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg22003010&aid=4] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg22003010&aid=4]

5. So now you will create a query that will only get you the tables where the TABSCHEMA equals the schema you used to create the octigate tables. In my case its db2inst1 so I will use this query to see only the tables owned by the db2inst1 schema userid.

[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg22003010&aid=5] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg22003010&aid=5]

6. So now that you have all the tables with the db2inst1 schema userid from the SYSIBMADM.ADMINTABINFO system table, you can now write a query that will calculate the total physical disk space and dump it to a file sorted by the highest usage. The total physical size is the sum of all the DATA, INDEX, LONG, LOB and XML object physical sizes.

The db2 command to do that is as shown here (in case you want to cut-n-paste). Make sure your DB2 Schema userid is correct, the example shown here is db2inst1.

db2 "SELECT SUBSTR(TABSCHEMA,1,15) as SCHEMA, SUBSTR(TABNAME,1,20) as TABLENAME,
> DATA_OBJECT_P_SIZE ,INDEX_OBJECT_P_SIZE , LONG_OBJECT_P_SIZE, LOB_OBJECT_P_SIZE,
> XML_OBJECT_P_SIZE, (DATA_OBJECT_P_SIZE + INDEX_OBJECT_P_SIZE + LONG_OBJECT_P_SIZE +
> LOB_OBJECT_P_SIZE + XML_OBJECT_P_SIZE ) as TOTAL_P_SIZE
> from SYSIBMADM.ADMINTABINFO 
> where TABSCHEMA='DB2INST1' 
> order by TOTAL_P_SIZE desc" >> /tmp/db2sizes.sorted.out

[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg22003010&aid=6] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg22003010&aid=6]

7. Open up the /tmp/db2sizes.out file and you will now see the total physical size (see the last column) sorted in descending order. This gives you an idea of your top tables that consume the most disk space. I've also attached my db2sizes.out file here from my test system if you want to take a look.
[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg22003010&aid=7] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg22003010&aid=7]

Note: This was written for the ITCAM Managing Server octigate database but you can use the same methodology to look at your other DB2 databases. For example, the ITM Tivoli Data Warehouse (TDW) historical database will be a good one to check disk usage at it usually stores larger amounts of historical data.

db2sizes.sorted.out [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg22003010&aid=9]db2sizes.sorted.out [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg22003010&aid=8]

PRODUCT ALIAS/SYNONYM
 ITCAM ITCAM4AD ITCAMfAD
response:
To alleviate performance problems with the octigate database in ITCAM, you can take the following steps:

1. Regularly trim data from the octigate database tables using the datatrim script provided. This will help reduce the size of tables over time.

2. Monitor and analyze the disk space usage of the octigate database tables. You can use the provided query to calculate the total physical disk space occupied by each table and identify the tables consuming the most disk space.

3. If the TEXTSTRING table is growing in size and causing performance issues, consider using the TextTrimmer tool for Windows to trim data from this table.

4. For migrating the octigate database from DB2 to Oracle, refer to the provided document DB2toOracle.doc for guidance on the migration process.

5. Optimize the collection of Method data by setting the MOD level to L3 for detailed data collection, but be mindful of the performance impacts and switch back to lower levels when not needed.

6. Utilize available tools like SchemaSpy to create a schema listing of all the octigate database tables for better understanding and management.

By following these steps, you can effectively address performance issues and optimize the performance of the octigate database in ITCAM.
overall_supported_explanation: The answer's suggestions on using the data trim script, monitoring disk space, dealing with the TEXTSTRING table, and optimizing data collection methods are supported by Document 0, Document 1, and Document 4. The suggestion of migration in Document 2 isn't directly relevant to alleviating current performance issues since it's more related to targeted database shifts. The use of SchemaSpy from Document 3 isn't covered in the response, though it's potentially useful for database schema analysis.
relevance_explanation: Document 0 provides a detailed methodology for managing disk space in the octigate database which helps alleviate performance issues. It mentions using scripts to trim data and monitor disk space usage. Document 1 discusses the TEXTSTRING table specifically, and mentions its rapid growth and the need for a special trimming tool. Document 4 explains the method of collecting Method data and its impact on performance, which can be related to alleviating performance issues by managing data collection levels.

question: What TLS cipherspecs/ciphersuites are supported when connecting from Oracle Java (non-IBM JRE) to MQ queue manager? What TLS cipherspecs/ciphersuites are supported when connecting from Oracle Java (non-IBM JRE) to MQ queue manager?
documents:
FIXES ARE AVAILABLE
WebSphere MQ V7.0 Fix Pack 7.0.1.4 [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg24028106]
WebSphere MQ V7.0.1 for i5/OS Fix Pack 7.0.1.4 [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg24028109]


SUBSCRIBE
You can track all active APARs for this component.



APAR STATUS
 * CLOSED AS PROGRAM ERROR.
    
   
   

ERROR DESCRIPTION
 *  Using a Server-Requester channel to send a message from one
   WebSphere MQ v7 queue manager to another v7 queue manager fails
   with the following error:
   AMQ6047: Conversion not supported.
   Explanation:
   WebSphere MQ is unable to convert string data tagged in
   CCSID 819 to data in CCSID 932.
   
   These are supported CCSID's according to the WebSphere MQ
   v7 Application Programming Reference Manual.
   
   The following FDC's are generated:
   Probe Id          :- JD514000
   Component         :- DmQueueManager.onError
   Process Name      :- D:\IBM\MQ\java\jre\bin\javaw.exe
   UserApp           :- TRUE
   Major Errorcode   :- MQRCCF_CCSID_ERROR
   .
   Probe Id          :- XC006001
   Component         :- xcsFreeMemFn
   Process Name      :- D:\IBM\MQ\java\jre\bin\javaw.exe
   UserApp           :- TRUE
   Major Errorcode   :- xecS_I_PRIVATE_MEMORY_ERROR
   Probe Description :- AMQ6091
   Arith1            :- 655711848 27155e68
   Comment1          :- invalid tail tag
   .
   Probe Id          :- ZT372000
   Component         :- zrfPrepareMsgForBackLevel
   Process Name      :- D:\IBM\MQ\bin\amqrmppa.exe
   UserApp           :- FALSE
   Major Errorcode   :- xecX_E_CONV_NOT_SUP
   Probe Type        :- MSGAMQ6047
   
   The third FDC, ZT372000 / zrfPrepareMsgForBackLevel, shows
   that WebSphere MQ is trying to prepare a message for sending to
   a pre-v7 queue manager. In this case however, both queue
   managers are v7.
   
   Additional keywords:
   CCSID codepage conversion
   
   
    
   
   

LOCAL FIX
 *  Use a SENDER-REVEIVER channel to ensure the WMQ v7-v7
   communication is as efficient as possible.
   
   
    
   
   

PROBLEM SUMMARY
 *  ****************************************************************
   USERS AFFECTED:
   All users of Server-Requester channels talking from a V7.0.*
   queue manager to another V7.0.* queue manager.
   
   Platforms affected:
   All Distributed (iSeries, all Unix and Windows)
   ****************************************************************
   PROBLEM SUMMARY:
   During the initial negotiation of a channel, parameters are
   agreed by both ends on what attributes the channel will have.
   
   When the channel which initiated the connection did not also
   initiate the negotiation phase (which is the case in a server
   to receiver setup) then a particular attribute was not being
   set correctly. This attribute controls whether the channel
   supports the new V7 message properties, and because it did not
   get set then when the message tried to flow it is incorrectly
   converted into a format understandable by pre-MQ v7.0 releases.
   
   In the normal case, for example sender to receiver, then the
   initiator of the channel also initiates the negotiation and
   this problem does not occur.
   
   
    
   
   

PROBLEM CONCLUSION
 *  WebSphere MQ has been modified to correctly negotiate the
   attribute in question, meaning a v7 server to v7 receiver setup
   no longer converts into pre-v7 format when transferring a
   message.
   
   Note this change also prevents the need for conversion to
   occur, however the conversion error itself is addressed under
   APAR IC64545 [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg1IC64545].
   
   ---------------------------------------------------------------
   The fix is targeted for delivery in the following PTFs:
   
                      v7.0
   Platform           Fix Pack 7.0.1.4
   --------           --------------------
   Windows            U200323
   AIX                U835793
   HP-UX (PA-RISC)    U836458
   HP-UX (Itanium)    U836463
   Solaris (SPARC)    U836459
   Solaris (x86-64)   U836465
   iSeries            tbc_p700_0_1_4
   Linux (x86)        U836460
   Linux (x86-64)     U836464
   Linux (zSeries)    U836461
   Linux (Power)      U836462
   
   The latest available maintenance can be obtained from
   'WebSphere MQ Recommended Fixes'
   http://www-1.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?rs=171&uid=swg27006037 [http://www-1.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?rs=171&uid=swg27006037]
   
   If the maintenance level is not yet available, information on
   its planned availability can be found in 'WebSphere MQ
   Planned Maintenance Release Dates'
   http://www-1.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?rs=171&uid=swg27006309 [http://www-1.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?rs=171&uid=swg27006309]
   ---------------------------------------------------------------
   
   
    
   
   

TEMPORARY FIX

COMMENTS

response:
The TLS cipherspecs/ciphersuites supported when connecting from Oracle Java (non-IBM JRE) to MQ queue manager are:
- RC4_MD5_EXPORT

It is important to note that IBM may need to deprecate the use of weaker algorithms in response to a security vulnerability, so it is recommended to review the MQ CipherSpecs available in the documentation provided.

documents:
sqlite TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)

RHEL 5.10 upgrade fails with the following errors:

CAUSE
The rhel 5.8 jump disk, nz-hostmgmt-v5.3.6.0_Disk8.iso, does not update sqlite to the current version needed for 5.10 to install, sqlite-3.3.6-5.

ENVIRONMENT

nz-hostmgmt-v5.3.6.0_Disk8.iso

RTC 100123 opened for this issue as well.

DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
When running the following commands you will see the following errors:

[root@ha1 U510_upgrade]# ./packageupgrade.sh
Installing RH 5.10 dependecies .......
Install RHEL5.10 package upgrades
warning: autofs-5.0.1-0.rc2.183.el5.x86_64.rpm: Header V3 DSA signature: NOKEY, key ID 37017186

sqlite >= 3.3.6-5 is needed by mkinitrd-5.1.19.6-81.el5_10.x86_64



RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Download nz-hostmgmt-v4.4.tar.gz or nz-hostmgmt-v4.4.iso to the hosts. 

Here is the generated link to the Fix Central page.

http://www.ibm.com/support/fixcentral/swg/quickorder?parent=ibm%7EInformation%2BManagement&product=ibm/Information+Management/Netezza+Platform&release=HOSTMGMT_4.4&platform=Linux&function=fixId&fixids=4.4.0.0-IM-Netezza-HOSTMGMT-fp16645&includeRequisites=1&includeSupersedes=0&downloadMethod=http&source=fc [http://www.ibm.com/support/fixcentral/swg/quickorder?parent=ibm%7EInformation%2BManagement&product=ibm/Information+Management/Netezza+Platform&release=HOSTMGMT_4.4&platform=Linux&function=fixId&fixids=4.4.0.0-IM-Netezza-HOSTMGMT-fp16645&includeRequisites=1&includeSupersedes=0&downloadMethod=http&source=fc]

Extract the following files from the RH55_upgrade directory:

sqlite-3.3.6-5.i386.rpm
sqlite-3.3.6-5.x86_64.rpm

Test and install them. 

See example:

rpm -Uvh sqlite-3.3.6-5.i386.rpm --test
then...
rpm -Uvh sqlite-3.3.6-5.i386.rpm

Then cd to the directory for RH510_upgrade and test to see if dependencies are met:

rpm -Uvh *.rpm --test

If all passes then install RHEL 5.10...

root @ U510_upgrade]# ./packageupgrade.sh

Package should install normally.

question: Why do we see database errors "caused by com.ibm.db2.jcc.am.SqlDataException: DB2 SQL Error: SQLCODE=-302, SQLSTATE=22001,..." in SystemOut.log? Why do we see database errors "caused by com.ibm.db2.jcc.am.SqlDataException: DB2 SQL Error: SQLCODE=-302, SQLSTATE=22001,..." in SystemOut.log?
documents:
TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 Unable to open jobs in DataStage. 
Opening a job fails with a popup window showing the error message "Unable to load RID - <id string>"

In the Websphere Application Server log (systemOut.log) you may see several errors similar to the following:
CNTR0020E: EJB threw an unexpected (non-declared) exception during invocation of method "removeEvents" on bean "BeanId(ACS_server.ear#ACS_server.jar#LoggingQueryService, null)". Exception data: javax.ejb.EJBException: nested exception is: com.ibm.db2.jcc.c.SqlException: DB2 SQL error: SQLCODE: -968, SQLSTATE: 57011, SQLERRMC: null
com.ibm.db2.jcc.c.SqlException: DB2 SQL error: SQLCODE: -968, SQLSTATE: 57011, SQLERRMC: null


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
The listed DB2 failure code occurs when the DB2 database is out of disk space or the database transaction logs are full. 

Your database administrator should look at the db2diag.log. The file is located on DB2 server , 
directory <db2instance_home>/sqllib/db2dump

If it contains an error stating disk is full, you will need to free up or add disk space to the database volume used by the DB2 xmeta database.

If there is no disk space error, check for additional errors. This error can also occur when the DB2 transaction logs are full.
response: We see these database errors in the SystemOut.log because there are issues related to data exceeding the allowable range in the database columns. The error SQLCODE=-302, SQLSTATE=22001 indicates that the value of an input host variable is found to be out of range for its corresponding use, leading to the SqlDataException and subsequent database errors. In one case, the error was related to the WORK_KEY column in the DISPATCHED_WORK_KEYS table not having enough capacity for the concatenated values. In another case, the SMISSubProfiles value was repeatedly concatenated, making it too large to fit into the expected database field. These issues caused the SQL errors to be logged in the SystemOut.log.
overall_supported_explanation: The response is generally supported by information found in the documents. Specifically, Document 1 and Document 4 offer in-depth explanations of situations that can lead to SQLCODE=-302 errors due to data values exceeding allowed ranges for database columns; however, not all parts of the response are completely verified by specific sentences. The overall narrative and examples provided align well with the claims in the response.
relevance_explanation: Document 1 and Document 4 provide concrete examples and explanations that address the query about SQLCODE=-302, SQLSTATE=22001 errors. Specifically, Document 1 details issues with data range in WORK_KEY column and Document 4 describes how repeated concatenation of the SMISSubProfiles value caused the same error. Document 2, despite mentioning SQLCODE=-302, refers to problems with software configurations rather than database column range issues and is therefore less directly relevant. Document 0 and Document 3 do not discuss SQLCODE=-302 or related scenarios, focusing instead on different types of database or software problems.

documents:
SECURITY BULLETIN

SUMMARY
 The MD5 "SLOTH" vulnerability on TLS 1.2 affects IBM Notes. 

VULNERABILITY DETAILS
 

CVEID: CVE-2015-7575 [http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2015-7575]
DESCRIPTION: The TLS protocol could allow weaker than expected security caused by a collision attack when using the MD5 hash function for signing a ServerKeyExchange message during a TLS handshake. An attacker could exploit this vulnerability using man-in-the-middle techniques to impersonate a TLS server and obtain credentials. 

 * 
 * 
 * 
 * 
 * https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/109415
 * 
 * 
 * 
 * 
 * 

AFFECTED PRODUCTS AND VERSIONS
 IBM Notes 9.0.1 Fix Pack 3 Interim Fix 2 through 9.0.1 Fix Pack 5.

NOTE: Since this vulnerability involves TLS 1.2, neither IBM Notes 9.0 nor IBM Notes 8.5.x are affected.

REMEDIATION/FIXES

The Notes SLOTH vulnerability is tracked as SPR KLYHA5YRVP and a fix is introduced in the following releases:


 * IBM Notes 9.0.1 Fixpack 5 IF 2 (for Windows) 
 * IBM Notes 9.0.1 Fixpack 5 IF 1 (for Linux) 
 * IBM Notes 64-bit 9.0.1 IF1 (for Mac 64-bit platform)


For Fix download links, refer to the following technote:  * Notes & Domino 9.0.1 Fix Packs and Interim Fixes - http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21657963 [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21657963]


Customers who remain on the following releases may open a Service Request with IBM Support [https://www-947.ibm.com/support/servicerequest/Home.action] and reference SPR KLYHA5YRVP for a custom hotfix:  * IBM Notes 9.0.1 Fix Pack 3 IF3 and later 
 * IBM Notes 9.0.1 Fix Pack 4 and all Interim Fixes


You should verify applying this fix does not cause any compatibility issues. The fix disables MD5 signature hash by default. If you change the default setting after applying the fix, you will expose yourself to the attack described above. IBM recommends that you review your entire environment to identify other areas where you have enabled the MD5 signature hash and take appropriate mitigation and remediation GET NOTIFIED ABOUT FUTURE SECURITY BULLETINS

question:
Error #2070 I purchased the SPSS grad pack and put one of the licenses on one computer and another on this one. This computer crashed and I was forced to reformat the hard drive. Subsequently, I lost SPSS in the process. After reinstalling the program, when I attempted to open a data set I received this error:
Error #2070 The application is unable to communicate properly with the license server. The license server may be down, or there may be other network-related communication or DNS issues interfering. This command is not executed. Specific symptom number: 3.  WHat is causing this?
documents:
TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 I am using an IBM SPSS Statistics product. My product is configured to use a network license. When I start the Statistics program I am receiving an error message: 'A fatal error has occurred and the client can no longer communicate with the server.' The Statistics program opens. The 'Output1' window indicates a licensing error and the 'IBM SPSS Statistics processor is unavailable' message appears near the bottom of the Statisics window. I cannot use the program. How can I fix this? 

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
There are multiple causes for this behavior.

The most common causes of this error are:

1) Incompatibility between the version of the Sentinel License RMS Licene Manager technology of the client and server.
2) Incorrect permissions settings on the local computer or user account.

If this error appears as a result of (1), the problem can be corrected by updating to the most recent version of Sentinel RMS License Manager version whenever updating/upgrading the IBM SPSS products licensed by a License Manager.

If this error appears as the result of (2), then applying the information below regarding the proper setting of Windows folder/file/registry permissions should correct the problem.

To run the Statistics program on a Microsoft Windows operating system requires a user account with the following settings -- for both operating system version and product version:

***Windows XP - Folder Permissions***
C:\Windows\system32 - Write
C:\Windows\Temp - Write
C:\Documents and Settings\All Users\Application Data\SafeNet Sentinel - Modify
SPSS product installation directory - Modify

***Windows Vista & later - Folder Permissions***
C:\Windows\system32 - Write
C:\Windows\Temp - Write
C:\ProgramData\SafeNet Sentinel - Modify
Statistics product installation directory - Modify

***Statistics 15 or earlier - Registry Permissions***
HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\SPSS - Full Control
HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\SPSS - Full Control
HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Ntpad - Full Control
HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Rainbow Technologies - Full Control

***Statistics 16 and later - Registry Permissions***
HKEY_CURRENT_USER\SOFTWARE\JavaSoft\Prefs - Full Control
HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\JavaSoft\Prefs - Full Control
HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Ntpad - Full Control
HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Rainbow Technologies - Full Control
HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\SPSS - Full Control
HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\IBM (19.0 and higher only) - Modify



HISTORICAL NUMBER
 87563
response: The error #2070 with specific symptom number 3 is caused by network-related communication or DNS issues interfering with the application's ability to communicate with the license server. This can be due to changes in network settings, firewall configurations, or DNS resolution problems that prevent the SPSS program from accessing the license server. In this specific case, reformatting the computer may have altered network settings or configurations, leading to the communication issue.

question: How to check IBM Java ORB build version in WebSphere Application Server? Just a simple question: How to check IBM Java ORB build version in WebSphere Application Server?
documents:
firewall; orb ; iiop ; launchclient ; waswlm; listener ; dynamic ; port TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 You access a server beyond a firewall and notice the ORB listener port is selected dynamically. 

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
IBM WebSphere Application Servers use various ports during operation for communication. The ORB uses a listener port for IIOP communications. By default, this listener port is selected dynamically during runtime. 
When working with a firewall, you must open a range of ports. You might want to instead open one port for the ORB to use. To do this, you must specify a static port for the ORB listener, and open the port specified on your firewall. 
The endPoint property for setting the ORB listener port is: 


 * ORB_LISTENER_ADDRESS 


You can specify this endpoint in serverindex.xml, located in the config directory. 
In the Network Deployment (ND) environment, the ORB_LISTENER_ADDRESS is always specified on the NodeAgent. The LSD (Location Service Daemon) resides on the NodeAgent and uses the same port as the ORB listener, resulting in the need to have that port fixed as well. 

Add the ORB_LISTENER_ADDRESS to the other Application Servers to set their ORB listen port. 

N ote: Each ORB must use a distinct listener port. If you are in an ND environment, you must specify a unique listener port for each entity on the same host. For example: 
 * NodeAgent: ORB_LISTENER_ADDRESS=9000 
 * Server1: ORB_LISTENER_ADDRESS=9811 
 * Server2: ORB_LISTENER_ADDRESS=9812 



From the Administrative Console : 
 * Select Servers > Application Servers > server1 > End Points. 
 * Click New. 
 * Select End Point Name as ORB_LISTENER_ADDRESS. 
 * Assign the port and hostname.



Cross reference information Segment Product Component Platform Version Edition Application Servers Runtimes for Java Technology Java SDK
response:
To check the IBM Java ORB build version in WebSphere Application Server, you can run the following command:

``` 
<washome>/java/bin/java -Xbootclasspath/p:<washome>/java/jre/lib/ext/ibmorb.jar com.ibm.rmi.util.Version
```

Replace `<washome>` with the actual path to your WebSphere Application Server installation directory. This command will help you determine the build version of the IBM Java ORB in your WebSphere Application Server environment.
overall_supported_explanation: The response is fully supported by the documents. Specifically, Document 1 provides the exact command needed to check the IBM Java ORB build version, which matches the command cited in the response. The response accurately summarizes the procedure to use the command including the path specification and the overall purpose of the command.
relevance_explanation: The information needed to answer the question on checking the IBM Java ORB build version in WebSphere Application Server is found in Document 1. Specifically, Document 1 provides a direct command to check the IBM Java ORB build version, which is essential for answering the query posed.

question: ITCAM Agent for DataPower  in TEP view, no data in the "HTTP Mean Transaction Times - Top 10" workspace. WIth ITCAM Agent for DataPower  in TEP view, there is no data in the "HTTP Mean Transaction Times - Top 10" workspace. The problem is that while the KBN agent is displaying some data in the TEP, in the "DataPower Appliances" view, there is no data in the "HTTP Mean Transaction Times - Top 10" workspace.
documents:
TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 The monitoring data sent from DataPower to ITCAM SOA and then displayed in Tivoli Enterprise Portal (TEP) includes the web service port name and namespace and the web service operation name and namespace. The monitoring data is stored by the ITCAM SOA data collector in flat files referred to as metric logs in the ITCAM SOA KD4\logs directory and sub-directories. 

In cases where the port and operation value might be customized by stylesheets in DataPower processing policies, the metric logs from ITCAM SOA, and the TEP may show the string 'UNKNOWN' or 'unknown' for Port Name or Operation Name. 

SYMPTOM
Here is the example of the metric log from ITCAM SOA for this issue. 

When the monitoring data is working properly, port and operation, and their respective namespaces, are included in the metric logs created by the ITCAM SOA data collector and in the TEP. 

For example, the HelloWorld WSDL, available in the DeveloperWorks article listed in the Related URL section, includes: 

 * targetNamespace="http://samples" 
 * <wsdl:portType name="Hello"> 
 *  <wsdl:operation name="sayHello">


A DataPower Web Service Proxy (WSP) based on HelloWorld.wsdl will generate monitoring data that includes the namespace http://samples, the port Hello, and the operation sayHello. When that data is collected by ITCAM SOA, it is included in the metric log, along with an epoch timestamp, e.g. 1330599364001 : 

 

 * 
 * 
 * 


In three cases, the monitoring data may include the string UNKNOWN for port and operation:  

 * 
 * 
 * 


The three cases where this issue may occur are:  * When the default Web Service Proxy monitoring data is customized with stylesheets in the processing policy for the WSP. 
 * When monitoring data is generated for a DataPower Multi-Protocol GateWay (MPGW) using custom stylesheets. 
 * When the service processing policy contains actions that generate additional records for ITCAM SOA data collection.


DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
Steps to determine root cause for UNKNOWN Port Name or Operation Name: 

 

 * Find the use case causing the issue:

 * 1. Search the metric logs in the ITCAM SOA KD4\logs\KD4CACHE\archive directory for "unknown" to find examples of the issue. A search tool such as Unix grep would yield results that include both the name of the metric log and the metric record itself; both are needed to help find the DataPower service that created the record. The records found will include an epoch timestamp and may contain a fault code and message that will help with finding root cause: 
   
   1330599364001; ... ;{}(unknown);{UNKNOWN}(unknown); ... ,fault_code;fault_message 
   
   2. Convert the epoch timestamp in the ITCAM SOA metric records to a readable timestamp. For example 1330599364001 is Thursday, March 01, 2012 5:56:04 AM GMT -5 (eastern). An option for converting the timestamp is to search the internet for "epoch" to find an epoch converter web site that can be used to convert the timestamp into a readable format. 
   
   3. Look at the name of the metric log where the "unknown" record was found. The name of the metric log usually indicates the appliance and the domain where the data originated. 
   
   4. Look in the DataPower logs that are being written off of the appliance for the same domain and the same time frame as the epoch timestamp. The DataPower log messages associated with the metric log will be simpler to find if a fault code and fault message from the metric log are also available to narrow the search. The DataPower messages associated with the metric log records will include a service name and transaction ID that will help clarify the use case causing the unknown value to appear in the metric records. 

 * Review the processing policies and the stylesheets in DataPower where monitoring data is being customized for the use case.

 *   1. Correct any stylesheet code where the operation or port is set without a namespace. For example, a good value for operation is {http://www.mycompany.com/abc}OperationName. If the operation element in the WSM record on DataPower appliance is set to just "OperationName" without the namespace {http://www.mycompany.com/abc} then {UNKNOWN} can result in the WSM record sent to ITCAM SOA. 
    2. Look for a processing policy where the use case identified above is not handled properly. For example, particularly if there is a fault code and fault message specified in the metric log record, look for an error condition that is not properly handled in an error rule. Look for, and correct, an error case where the port and operation values are not properly set. 
    3. Confirm the dp:wsm-agent-append() parameters in the stylesheets are correct. For example, a valid statement is: dp:wsm-agent-append($domain, $record/dpwsm:wsa-record) Confirm that XPATH dpwsm:wsa-record is included in the second parameter. For example, this this can create the {UNKNOWN} issue: dp:wsm-agent-append($domain, $record) 
    4. In the sample MPGW stylesheets, the port and operation values are set in a separate stylesheet from the dp:wsm-agent-append() that uses those values. Look for, and correct cases where the port and operation values are not set, and empty or invalid values are used in the dp:wsm-agent-append(). Use cases might include AAA or validation failures where DataPower rejects a message on the request side before the stylesheet that sets port and operation executes. Use the DataPower probe or add debug statements to stylesheet that includes the dp:wsm-agent-append(), add debug statements 
    5. The default value is the best value for the vast majority of monitoring data elements. Look for any stylesheet code that customizes fields that are not string values, such timestamps or numeric duration fields. Setting invalid data, for example alphabetic string data, in a timestamp or numeric field can cause an UNKNOWN value for port and operation. 
    6. The service processing policy may contain actions generate additional records for ITCAM SOA data collection and those records may have value UNKNOWN for the port namespace. Please see WebSphere DataPower processing actions that can affect message counts in ITCAM SOA http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21587632 [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21587632]
   
   

RELATED INFORMATION
#DeveloperWorks article with sample HelloWorld.wsdl atta [http://www.ibm.com/developerworks/webservices/library/ws-noide2/]
ITCAM for SOA Version 7.1.1 Configuring DataPower Proc [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/tivihelp/v3r1/index.jsp?topic=/com.ibm.itcamsoa.doc/kd4inmst148.htm]

question:
Can I remove the write permissions for "other" in ../ibm/common/acsi? Can I remove "write" permission for "other" for all the sub-directories and files under the following directory,

    /usr/ibm/common/acsi  
    /var/ibm/common/acsi  

Will it cause any problem?
documents:
Registry Entries Incorrect TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 CheckerException error thrown during DE upgrade due to older misleading DE entries appearing as active. 

SYMPTOM
Sometimes DE upgrade may fail due to previously existing DE stale entries.

In such cases , the following exception is thrown
Caused by: com.ibm.ac.si.checker.CheckerException:
ACUDCI0711E Dependency Checker determined that the following IUInstance:
SoftwareInstance\[9d964d42a90194d1:1c094808:127533028ae:-8000,RootIUTypeID[D94240D11C8B11D99F2D00061BDE7AEA,1.4.0.x]
,Install IU,mrid:[w3.ibm.com/namespaces/2003/OS_componentTypes:Operating_System,C:/Program] Files/IBM/Common/acsi,0\] cannot be created because another version of that instance already exists in the IURegistry 



RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Follow the below mentioned steps (in same order) to clean up the stale entries. 

On Windows : 


1) User has to run de_version command and listIU command. compare the DE entries. If entries are different it means there are stale entries, make changes to C:/Program Files/IBM/Common/acsi\dat\descriptors\SI_packagedIU.xml related to version, replace version element entries with DE version entries from listIU command output 


2)From C:/Program Files/IBM/Common/acsi/bin location, Run 

 #manageIU -o delete -r "C:/Program Files/IBM/Common/acsi" -p "C:/Program Files/IBM/Common/acsi\dat\descriptors\SI_packagedIU.xml" 


This command will remove stale entries. User will see success count 2 and errorcount 0 in the output


3) Once the delete operation is successful, modify C:/Program Files/IBM/Common/acsi\dat\descriptors\SI_packagedIU.xml file version entries with DE version value from de_version command output. 


4) Run command C:\Program Files\IBM\Common\acsi\bin\manageIU -o
create -r "C:/Program Files/IBM/common/acsi" -p "C:/Program Files/IBM/Common/acsi\dat\descriptors\SI_packagedIU.xml". User will see success count 2 and errorcount 0 in the output once create operation is successful 


5) Delete packagedIU.xml located at C:/Program Files/IBM/common/acsi/dat/descriptors 


6) At C:/Program Files/IBM/common/acsi/dat/descriptors, please check DE version from de_version command matches version entries in these files SI_packagedIU.xml, SIUpgrade_packagedIU.xml, if not replace version entries with version from de_version command. 


7) You may now proceed with your Product Installation 

 

On Unix platforms : 

1) User has to run de_version command and listIU command. compare the DE entries. If entries are different it means there are stale entries, make changes to /var/ibm/common/acsi\dat\descriptors\SI_packagedIU.xml related to version, replace version element entries with DE version entries from listIU command output 


2) From /usr/ibm/common/acsi/bin location, Run:

#manageIU -o delete -r "/usr/ibm/common/acsi" -p "/var/ibm/common/acsi\dat\descriptors\SI_packagedIU.xml" 

This command will remove stale entries. User will see success count 2 and errorcount 0 in the output

3) Once the delete operation is successful, modify /var/ibm/common/acsi\dat\descriptors\SI_packagedIU.xml file version entries with DE version value from de_version command output.

4) From /usr/ibm/common/acsi/bin location, Run command:

#manageIU -o create -r "/usr/ibm/common/acsi" -p "/var/ibm/common/acsi\dat\descriptors\SI_packagedIU.xml" 

User will see success count 2 and errorcount 0 in the output once create operation is successful

5) Delete packagedIU.xml located at /var/ibm/common/acsi/dat/descriptors

6) At /var/ibm/common/acsi/dat/descriptors, please check DE version from de_version command output matches version entries in the SI_packagedIU.xml if not replace version entries with version from de_version command.

7) You may now proceed with your Product Installation




PRODUCT ALIAS/SYNONYM
 DE
response: It is safe to remove the write permission for "other" in the directories `/usr/ibm/common/acsi` and `/var/ibm/common/acsi` and all their sub-directories and files. This action will not cause any problems and is recommended to enhance security by restricting write permissions for unauthorized users.
overall_supported_explanation: The response is supported by the documents. Document 4 explicitly states that it is safe to remove world writable permissions from directories including '/usr/ibm/common/acsi' which directly supports the claim in the response.
relevance_explanation: The question pertains to removing write permissions from directories and files under '/usr/ibm/common/acsi' and '/var/ibm/common/acsi'. Document 4 is directly related as it discusses managing permissions for directories and files within the IBM structure, specifically mentioning the ability to safely change permissions for directories associated with the Deployment Engine (DE) to enhance system security without impacting functionality. This makes Document 4 the most relevant to address the question.

question: Where can I find information on security issues addressed in WAS fix pack 8.5.5.6? I am planning to upgrade to WAS fix pack 8.5.5.6 and would like to know what security vulnerabilities, if any, are addressed in this fix pack.
documents:
SUBSCRIBE
You can track all active APARs for this component.



APAR STATUS
 * CLOSED AS PROGRAM ERROR.
    
   
   

ERROR DESCRIPTION
 *  ***Overview
   
   If the user group has "grant access" for "approve work order"
   checked in the options section then the status "waiting for
   material" is available, even if the security option "Set work
   order status to waiting for materials" is unticked.
   
   Reproduced the problem on Maximo 6.2.2 and 6.2.1.
   
   Checked with P&T and they response.
   
   
   /Bedford/IBM wrote on 17/10/2008 12:45:35 AM:
   
   > WMATL is a synonym of APPR so I can see why this might be
   happening.
   > I would call this a bug, but I'm not sure how we would fix it.
   I see
   > two options:
   > 1. We fix it so you can grant APPR, but not grant WMATL and
   this
   > would do what you are expectring it to do
   > or
   > 2. We remove the WMATL sig option because it is merely a
   synonym of
   > APPR. I don't know if we can set a sig option to control
   access to synonyms.
   
   >
   > Steve, is it possible to grant access to a base Maximo status
   like
   > APPR, but then NOT grant access to a synonym like WMATL?
   
   
   So adding a APAR.
   
   
   ****Steps to reproduce.
   
   1. created a group WMATL and user WMATLUSER they have access to
   work order application and at least one site.
   
   2. Uncheck the work order option
   "set Work Order status to Waiting for Materials".
   
   3. Make sure the option
   
   "grant access" for "approve work order"
   
   4. Create a workorder and attempt change the status to waiting
   materials.
   
   
   I found on Maximo 6.2.2 it would allow me to change the status
   to WMATL on a workorder, even though the security setting was
   switched off.
   
   
   
   
   Client using Maximo 6.2.2
   
   I reproduced this on Maximo 6.2.2 seems to be a bug or
   miss-reading what this permission allows.
   
   
    
   
   

LOCAL FIX

PROBLEM SUMMARY
 *  ****************************************************************
   * USERS AFFECTED: All Maximo users                             *
   ****************************************************************
   * PROBLEM DESCRIPTION: CHANGING STATUS TO WMATL was checking   *

   ****************************************************************

   *                                                              *
   *                                                              *
   *                                                              *
   ****************************************************************
   6.2.2 THE SECURITY OPTION "SET WORK ORDER STATUS TO WAITING FOR
   MATERIALS" DOESN'T PREVENT USERS FROM CHANGING STATUS TO WMATL
   
   
    
   
   

PROBLEM CONCLUSION
 *  <CHANGING STATUS TO WMATL to check sigoption  WMATL.>
   
   
   
   
   
   The fix for this APAR is contained in the following maintenance

CAUSE

http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg1IV38051 [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg1IV38051]

Check javacore or system dump at the time of the problem. The current thread would have fillInStackTrace in java callstack and jitHandleNullPointerExceptionTrap in native callstack.

response:
The issue of native out of memory errors on a 64-bit system could be caused by limitations in the system resources or configuration. The specific error message "Failed to create a thread: retVal -1073741830, errno 11" indicates that the Java process was unable to create a new thread, possibly due to a kernel-level limit being reached.

To troubleshoot and potentially resolve this issue, you should check the following:

1. Verify that there are no limitations on creating processes by checking the Node Agent settings, such as running as root or proper configuration for non-root users.
  
2. Check the ulimit settings for the maximum number of processes a user can create. You can use the command `ulimit -a` to check the current value and adjust it if necessary.

3. Ensure that the `maxuproc` kernel parameter is set to a high enough value. This parameter defines the maximum number of processes a user can have at a given time. You can check the current value with `lsattr -E -l sys0|grep maxuproc` and adjust it as needed using `chdev -l sys0 -a maxuproc='nnnn'`.

By addressing these potential limitations and adjusting the system settings, you may be able to prevent the native out of memory issues and the disappearance of the processID on your Portal servers in the clusters.
overall_supported_explanation: The response is generally well supported by the referenced documents, particularly Document 1 and Document 2. Both documents provide specifics regarding system configurations, kernel limitations and the use of system tools like ulimit, which align with the steps suggested in the response for checking and adjusting the settings to address native out of memory errors.
relevance_explanation: Document 1 and Document 2 are the most relevant to answer the question regarding native out of memory issues and their solutions on a 64-bit system. Document 1 mainly discusses problems linked to Information Services Director (ISD) running out of memory as it cannot create a new thread because of a kernel-level limit, explaining various system and configuration settings involved in such a scenario. Document 2 similarly delves into OutOfMemoryError messages and focuses on maximum heap size and native memory limitations, discussing the nproc settings and ulimit commands which are crucial for handling the number of processes and threads

question: In FileNet Workplace XT .csv files open as a text file instead of using MS Excel The .csv file was created through WorkplaceXT and it only can be opened as the text file instead of the Excel file, and the mime type is text/csv, not application/vnd.ms-excel. However, when creating the same .csv document through ACCE, the mime Type is "application/vnd.ms-excel" shown under the Properties tab and Content Element tab.
documents:
Mime type TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

THIS DOCUMENT APPLIES ONLY TO THE FOLLOWING LANGUAGE VERSION(S):
 US English 

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 The .csv file was created through WorkplaceXT and it only can be opened as the text file instead of the Excel file 

SYMPTOM
The .csv file can be opened as the Excel from ACCE and outside of WorkplaceXT but not from inside of the WorkplaceXT


CAUSE
Mime Type was not set correctly when creating the .csv documents via WorkplaceXT

DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
When creating a .csv document through ACCE, the mime Type is "application/vnd.ms-excel" shown under the Properties tab and Content Element tab. 

 * When adding a .csv document through WorkplaceXT, the mime Type shows "text/csv" from the Properties tab and Content Element tab in ACCE. 
 * Verify if the .csv document with the mime Type "application/vnd.ms-excel" is opened as Excel file in WorkplaceXT

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Add a section to Web.xml mapping the extension to the mime type: 

 * 
 * <mime-mapping>
   <extension>csv</extension>
   <mime-type>application/vnd.ms-excel</mime-type>
   </mime-mapping>
response: The issue with FileNet Workplace XT .csv files opening as a text file instead of using MS Excel is caused by the Mime Type not being set correctly when creating the .csv documents via WorkplaceXT. To resolve this problem, a section needs to be added to the Web.xml mapping the extension ".csv" to the mime type "application/vnd.ms-excel." This change will ensure that .csv files created through WorkplaceXT can be opened as Excel files instead of text files.
overall_supported_explanation: The response is fully supported by Document 0. The explanation of the issue and its causes are directly mentioned in Document 0, which addresses the problem of mime types affecting how files are opened in different environments. The solution provided in the response aligns with the resolution steps detailed in Document 0.
relevance_explanation: The relevant information for answering the question about .csv files being opened as text files in WorkplaceXT but as Excel files in ACCE comes from Document 0. This document explains that the mime type is set as 'text/csv' when .csv files are created in WorkplaceXT, and as 'application/vnd.ms-excel' in ACCE. Document 0 also provides the resolution for this issue by recommending changes to the Web.xml configuration.

question: Authorization failed.: The Authorization Code entered is not for this product/version. If you have multiple Authorization Codes, please check you have entered the correct one.
documents:
product key authorization license TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 I am attempting to license the SPSS Sentinel License Manager for my SPSS products. Entering the authorization code/license code produces an error:

Sentinel LM: Error[19]: Failed to add license code to the license server on host "". License code is invalid.

followed by...

Error Code: 30019

The License Code that you entered is not valid. Please enter a License Code from SPSS. Possibly you entered the Authorization Code that came with your product. If you did, click Back and use your Authorization Code to get a license from SPSS via the Internet, Telephone, or E-mail.

Lock Code:
Authorization Code:
License Code:

What is the problem? 

ANSWER
1. The Lock Code encrypted into the entered license does not match that of the hardware you are attempting to license. If you are unable to determine which server for which this license was generated, please contact IBM SPSS Customer Support [http://www.ibm.com/planetwide/region.html]. 

2. You are attempting to license your installation via a Terminal Services/Citrix session with a single or site authorization/license. If you intend to run the software via Terminal Services/Citrix, you will need to obtain a Network license. Please contact your local IBM Sales office [http://www.ibm.com/planetwide/region.html]. If you intend to run the application on a server-class machine without Terminal Services/Citrix or you are licensing a server based product, e.g. SPSS Server, please go to that machine directly and run the License Authorization Wizard to request/submit a license.

3. You are entering a network authorization code into the License Authorization Wizard installed with SPSS for Windows, SPSS Text Analysis for Surveys, or AMOS. The network authorization code is required to license the SPSS Sentinel License Manager, not the aforementioned applications. At this point, you must contact IBM SPSS Customer Support [http://www.ibm.com/planetwide/region.html] and request a new authorization as the network authorization code is only valid for one machine. Once you receive the new authorization code, please launch the License Authorization Wizard installed with the SPSS Sentinel License Manager (Start->Programs->SPSS Sentinel License Manager->SPSS License Authorization Wizard) and enter your authorization code when prompted.

4. Ensure the SPSS Sentinel License Manager service is started. Go to Start->Control Panel->Administrative Tools->Services and locate "Sentinel LM". The status should report the service is started. If it is not started, i.e. blank, right-click/double-click on the "Sentinel LM" service name and select "Start". If the service will not start, one possible cause could be the Sentinel License Manager was previously installed elsewhere, but has since been removed and installed into a different directory. If that is the case, go to Start->Programs->SPSS Sentinel License Manager->Service Loader. Next, ensure the "Executable Path" is correct and click "Add". Finally, start the "Sentinel LM" service following the aforementioned steps.

5. You have an lshost or lsforcehost environment variable defined on the machine where the SPSS Sentinel License Manager is installed. Please remove the environment variable and run the License Authorization Wizard again on this machine to request a license.

6. You are not logged in as 'root' while attempting to license the SPSS Sentinel License Manager on a non-Windows platform. Please log in as 'root' prior to licensing your installation

RELATED INFORMATION
 Need more help? Our Statistics forum is Live! [https://developer.ibm.com/answers/topics/statistics.html?smartspace=predictive-analytics]
Need more help? Our Licensing forum is Live! [https://developer.ibm.com/answers/topics/spsslicensing.html]


 

HISTORICAL NUMBER
 49341
response: The problem is that the Authorization Code entered is not for the correct product/version. If multiple Authorization Codes are available, it is recommended to double-check and ensure the correct one is entered.

question: Restore JazzSM DASH login page to default images We've changed the JazzSM DASH login page images and want to restore them. What values do we use to revert those changes?
documents:
DASHL2; validate; validation; credentials; primary admin user; JazzSM; TWS; DWC TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 The step to validate the admin user credentials when applying JazzSM 1.1.2.1 to a TWS/DWC 9.3/JazzSM 1.1.2 install hangs for several minutes and then fails indicating the credentials are incorrect. 

CAUSE
The validation check is failing to detect all application ports are active. It waits 5 minutes for all ports to become active and then times out. The TWS/DWC migration script "tdwcUpgrade" changes the default host name value for the "endPointName="IPC_CONNECTOR_ADDRESS" entry in the <JazzSM Home>/profile/config/cells/JazzSMNode01Cell/nodes/JazzSMNode01/serverindex.xml file from "${LOCALHOST_NAME}" to the fully qualified host name for the DASH server. That change affects how the port status appears in the netstat output. So, even though the port is active it does not appear active to the JazzSM installer.

DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
Review the serverindex.xml file for the host value specified for the "endPointName="IPC_CONNECTOR_ADDRESS" entry to see if it is set to the FQDN for the DASH host. 

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
TWS Support indicates the FQDN value is necessary. To address the issue the host value for the "endPointName="IPC_CONNECTOR_ADDRESS" entry in the <JazzSM Home>/profile/config/cells/JazzSMNode01Cell/nodes/JazzSMNode01/serverindex.xml can be changed to "localhost", and then retry the JazzSM 1.1.2.1 update. After the install is complete, the hostname value for the "endPointName="IPC_CONNECTOR_ADDRESS" entry can be set back to the DASH host's FQDN.

Note: The instance must be restarted for any changes to the serverindex.xml to take effect.
response:
To restore the JazzSM DASH login page to default images, you need to follow these steps:

1. Stop the DASH server.
2. Make a backup copy of the current xml files in the following directory: <JazzSM_Home>/profile/config/cells/JazzSMNode01Cell/applications/isc.ear/deployments/isc/isclite.war/WEB-INF
3. Go to <JazzSM_Home>/profile/backups and find a backup file from a previous day/time that contains the missing xml files.
4. Unpack the zip file to a temporary location.
5. Restore the xml files from the backup to the WEB-INF directory.
6. Start the DASH server.
7. Test if you can access the DASH Portal with the default login page images.
overall_supported_explanation: The response generally aligns with the procedures described in Document 3 for handling corrupted or missing XML files and using backups to restore them, relating this to the restoration of default settings implicitly, such as those for the login page. Each step in the response is generally supported by a corresponding action or recommendation in Document 3, though the specifics around image files or settings specifically for the "login page images" are not directly mentioned in the documents.
relevance_explanation: Document 3 contains useful information for answering the question regarding restoring default settings for the JazzSM DASH, specifically dealing with potential XML file corruption and restoring them from backup. This directly relates to the question of restoring default XML configuration files which might contain settings for the login page images.

question: Unable to locate the More tab of Document class - Property definition tab - property template configuration to update Is Required setting. Unable to locate the More tab of Document class - Property definition tab - property template configuration to update Is Required setting. Environment is running on Content Engine 5.2.0.3.
documents:
ACCE; CE; Property; FEM TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 With IBM FileNet Enterprise Manager (FEM), we can follow the steps below to overwrite the property definition in a document class

1. Launch FEM
2. Expand the object store and document class tree. Right click on the document class and select "Properties"
3. Go to "Properties Definitions" tab, highlight the property definition and click "Edit"
4. Make the modification and click OK
5. Click OK again to save the change

How to perform the same operation with FileNet Content Engine - ACCE? 

ANSWER
Follow the below steps to update the property definition properties on ACCE: 




 1. Launch ACCE 
 2. Expand the object stores folder and click the object store to open the Object store tab 
 3. Expand the Data Design folder and Classes folder. Click the document class to open Document Class tab 
 4. From Properties tab, open Property Definitions drop down and select the property definition you want to modify 
 5. ACCE will open that property definition in a new Properties tab 
 6. Modify the property definition as required 
 7. Go back to the Class Definition tab and click Save
response:
Because the environment is running on Content Engine 5.2.0.3, you will not find the "More" tab in the Document class - Property definition tab - property template configuration to update the "Is Required" setting. The "More" tab was removed starting with Content Platform Engine (CPE) 5.2.1. 

To update the "Is Required" setting in this environment, you will need to follow a different process. Here are the steps:

1. Bring up ACCE
2. Navigate to the Object Stores tree and click on the object store to open the object store tab
3. Navigate to the Data Design and Classes folder, expand the Document Class, and select the document class where you want to update the "Is Required" setting to open the Document Class tab
4. From the Properties tab, select the property definition you want to modify
5. ACCE will open that property definition in a separate tab
6. Modify the property definition as required directly in the properties tab without the need for the "More" tab
7. Once you have made the necessary changes, go back to the Class Definition tab and click Save for the changes to take effect.
overall_supported_explanation: The response aligns well with the details provided in Document 4, which discusses the UI changes and the removal of the 'More' tab starting with version CPE 5.2.1, exactly matching the scenario mentioned in Content Engine 5.2.0.3 in the response. Furthermore, the steps outlined in the response to adjust properties within the ACCE interface correspond closely to those detailed in Document 4 for handling choice lists, which suggests a similarity in steps for different settings adjustments within ACCE.
relevance_explanation: Document 4 is primarily relevant as it directly discusses the removal of the 'More' tab in various versions of CPE, including 5.2.1, and explains the steps needed to modify properties in ACCE in versions where FEM is absent. These discuss the changes and processes that are relevant to the issue of missing tabs and differences in UI that might hinder finding the 'Is Required' setting feature.

SYMPTOM
The Oracle database adapter log (.dbl) reports the following error:

Unsupported datatype returned is being treated as text.


CAUSE
SYS_REFCURSOR is not a valid datatype as a return from an Oracle stored procedure call using the WTX / ITX Oracle adapter.

ENVIRONMENT
IBM WebSphere TX / IBM TX Oracle adapter on any valid execution platform

DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
LASTERRORMSG() reports 'Failed to execute the SQL statement' and the database adapter log reports 'Unsupported datatype returned is being treated as text.'

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Redesign the Stored Procedure to NOT return a SYS_REFCURSOR datatype.
response:
To call a stored procedure in the input card of a map using WTX, you can follow these steps:

1. Create a temporary working table in Oracle to store the results of the stored procedure call. For example, you can create a table named `TEMPWORK`.

2. Let the stored procedure write the results of its operations to the `TEMPWORK` table.

3. Create a sub-report (or a new report) based on the `TEMPWORK` table.

4. In a multi-user environment, distinguish the records written by a given user by adding a new column (for example, `USRID`) to the `TEMPWORK` table. Populate the `USRID` column in the stored procedure using the `USER` function.

5. Filter the table in the Impromptu sub-report using the User ID Impromptu function (`USRID=User Id`).

Additionally, you can use Oracle session information to further identify the records belonging to a particular session by adding a `SESSID` column to the `TEMPWORK` table and setting it using the `USERENV('SESSIONID')` function.

Remember to create a view on this information in the database to filter on the `SESSID` column in Impromptu.

You can refer to the section on "How can I get Oracle stored procedures to return data to Impromptu?" in the provided documents (72018 and 88300) for a more detailed step-by-step example and implementation.

Hope this helps!

question: Recurrent RES StaleConnectionException How to prevent rule execution failing because of recurrent StaleConnectionException on ODM 8.8 Rule Execution Server?
documents:
ilog.rules.res.tools.http.IlrDeployRuleAppException; ilog.rules.res.tools.http.IlrRemoteConnection.deployRuleAppArchiveWithXOM(IlrRemoteConnection.java:150); Caused by: java.sql.SQLDataException: A truncation error was encountered trying to shrink VARCHAR 'resuri://..; resuri://a..; r&' to length 2000. ... 45 more TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 Why do I get an error message "IlrDeployRuleAppException: Error during the import of RuleApps" when deploying a RuleApp to Rule Execution Server. 

CAUSE
When you deploy a RuleApp, the referenced JAR files in the RuleApp are listed as a URI under the property "ruleset.managedxom.uris". This property is stored as a VARCHAR inside the database. Databases have a set limit on the number of characters in the VARCHAR data type, therefore the exception is thrown if the URI exceeds this limit.

ANSWER
To solve this problem, you can use the XOM as Library in the Rule Execution Server console. 


The first step is to disable the export of the referenced XOM JAR libraries from the rule project to the XOM Path, from which the RuleApp is generated. This creates an empty XOM Path Entry, which allows you to deploy the RuleApp to Rule Execution Server. 

To prevent the export of the libraries to the XOM Path:


 1. In Rule Designer, in the Rule Explorer view, right-click the rule project, and click Properties. 
 2. Click Java Execution Object Model in the list of properties. 
 3. To display a list of all selected Java XOM entries, click the Order and Export tab. 
 4. Clear the check boxes that correspond to the libraries, and then click OK.


Once the RuleApp is deployed, you must add the XOM as a library URI in the Rule Execution Server console. 

To add the XOM as a library URI: 

1. Deploy the XOM to Rule Execution Server, using "Deploy Resource" in the Rule Execution Server console. 
For more information on how to deploy a resource, see:
http://pic.dhe.ibm.com/infocenter/dmanager/v7r5/index.jsp?topic=%2Fcom.ibm.dserver.res%2FContent%2FBusiness_Rules%2F_pubskel%2FInfocenter_Primary%2Fps_DS_RES_Console_OH1668.html [http://pic.dhe.ibm.com/infocenter/dmanager/v7r5/index.jsp?topic=%2Fcom.ibm.dserver.res%2FContent%2FBusiness_Rules%2F_pubskel%2FInfocenter_Primary%2Fps_DS_RES_Console_OH1668.html] 

Deploying a large number of XOM files to Rule Execution Server can get cumbersome. To speed up the task, use the ANT task "res-deploy-xom" if possible. 
For more information on how to use the res-deploy-xom Ant task, see:
http://pic.dhe.ibm.com/infocenter/dmanager/v7r5/topic/com.ibm.dserver.reference.res/Content/Business_Rules/_pubskel/Infocenter_Primary/ps_DS_Reference_RES2064.html [http://pic.dhe.ibm.com/infocenter/dmanager/v7r5/topic/com.ibm.dserver.reference.res/Content/Business_Rules/_pubskel/Infocenter_Primary/ps_DS_Reference_RES2064.html] 

2. After deploying the XOM, add a Library as described in the following documentation: 

http://pic.dhe.ibm.com/infocenter/dmanager/v7r5/index.jsp?topic=%2Fcom.ibm.dserver.res%2FContent%2FBusiness_Rules%2F_pubskel%2FInfocenter_Primary%2Fps_DS_RES_Console_OH1675.html [http://pic.dhe.ibm.com/infocenter/dmanager/v7r5/index.jsp?topic=%2Fcom.ibm.dserver.res%2FContent%2FBusiness_Rules%2F_pubskel%2FInfocenter_Primary%2Fps_DS_RES_Console_OH1675.html] 

3. After adding a Library, add the deployed XOM to this library, as described in the following documentation:
http://pic.dhe.ibm.com/infocenter/dmanager/v7r5/index.jsp?topic=%2Fcom.ibm.dserver.res%2FContent%2FBusiness_Rules%2F_pubskel%2FInfocenter_Primary%2Fps_DS_RES_Console_OH1676.html [http://pic.dhe.ibm.com/infocenter/dmanager/v7r5/index.jsp?topic=%2Fcom.ibm.dserver.res%2FContent%2FBusiness_Rules%2F_pubskel%2FInfocenter_Primary%2Fps_DS_RES_Console_OH1676.html] 

4. Add the "ruleset.managedxom.uris" property to the deployed RuleApp and point it to the newly created library. You must specify the name of the library in the format: "reslib://NewLibrary", where "NewLibrary" is the name of the library that you just added.
response:
To prevent rule execution from failing because of recurrent StaleConnectionException on ODM 8.8 Rule Execution Server, you can follow these steps:

1. Configure the WebSphere Application Server data source connection pool to eliminate occurrences of StaleConnectionException:
   - Set the Minimum connections to 0 to avoid situations where a connection is kept in the pool to meet the minimum requirement, even though it may have been idle for longer than Unused timeout.
   - Set the values for Unused timeout and Reap timeout so that the sum does not exceed any network level idle connection timeout.

2. Check the network connection for the data source to ensure it remains valid even after a period of inactivity. A firewall between the Application Server and the Database may have a policy to discard network connections that remain idle for some time, causing the StaleConnectionException.

By following these steps, you can prevent recurrent StaleConnectionExceptions and ensure smooth rule execution on the ODM 8.8 Rule Execution Server.
overall_supported_explanation: The response is mostly supported by Document 2, which contains relevant information about handling StaleConnectionExceptions. Sentences 2m-n-o provide specific configuration settings which match those proposed in the response to prevent these errors. However, the response misses deeper insights offered by Document 2 regarding causes such as network policies by firewalls that could impact the data connection validity.
relevance_explanation: Document 2 directly addresses the issue of StaleConnectionException, which is the subject of the question. Specifically, sentences 2b through 2g discuss the problem and its cause, while sentences 2m through 2o provide solutions for preventing the exception, primarily via settings adjustments in the WebSphere Application Server data source connection pool. No other documents directly address the StaleConnectionException or provide relevant data or solutions specific to this problem in the context of Rule Execution Server and ODM.

question:
Product upgrade installed a second instance of the DE, causing upgrade failure. The Deployment Engine should only have one instance installed on a server with IBM products installed. In this case, a second DE was installed, which caused the failure of the Product upgrade. Error: Wrong Deployment Engine.

Product install failed. You will get a message similar to this: You are updating an instance of (Product name) that was installed using the Tivoli Deployment Engine (DE) located at "/home/nc_admin/.acsi_FQDN". However you are now using the DE located at "/home/nc_admin/.acsi_HOSTNAME".

When the DE is installed as non-root ( in this case nc_admin ), it will install the DE in the user home directory, in this case /home/nc_admin, and use the FQDN in the name: /home/nc_admin/.acsi_FQDN
documents:
DEL2INST; install fails ACUINI0061I; user authority TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 Installation of Deployment Engine fails with below errors:
com.ibm.ac.si.install.InstallUnauthorizedException: ACUINI0061I User, 1054 does not have root authority!
at com.ibm.ac.si.install.SIInstaller.validateUnixUserAuthority(SIInstaller.java:1997) 

SYMPTOM
You may see below errors in DEInstall.log and/or de_trace.log when installing an application. These error suggest that DE was attempting to install as root, although user could be a non-root user:


com.ibm.ac.si.install.InstallUnauthorizedException: ACUINI0061I User, 1054 does not have root authority!
at com.ibm.ac.si.install.SIInstaller.validateUnixUserAuthority(SIInstaller.java:1997)
at com.ibm.ac.si.install.SIInstaller.validateUserAuthority(SIInstaller.java:1921)
at com.ibm.ac.si.install.SIInstaller.setInstallMode(SIInstaller.java:6023)
at com.ibm.ac.si.install.SIInstaller.initialize(SIInstaller.java:2343)
at com.ibm.ac.si.install.SIInstaller.<init>(SIInstaller.java:2219)
at com.ibm.ac.si.install.SIInstaller.<init>(SIInstaller.java:2187)
at com.ibm.ac.si.install.SIInstaller.<init>(SIInstaller.java:2175)
at com.ibm.ac.si.install.SIInstaller.getInstalledSIClasspath(SIInstaller.java:1410)
at com.ibm.ac.coi.ext.ia.DEBootstrapInstall.install(DEBootstrapInstall.java:319)
at com.ibm.ac.coi.ext.ia.DEBootstrapWorkerThread.run(DEBootstrapWorkerThread.java:253)


CAUSE
This error can be a result of an improperly created non-root user account on Solaris platform. If a non-root user is added using simplified command syntax (eg; useradd -m testuser), no directory will be created in /home or /export/home on Solaris. But Solaris will set the $HOME variable as /home/<username> (<username> refers to the id of the non-root user being created). 

Solaris will regard /export/home/<username> as user's home directory. Since this directory does not exist so DE may fail to install the application.


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
1. Installation being performed as non-root user:


If user's home directory does not exist and installation is being performed as a non-root user then create user's home directory and set HOME variable to that directory. Then perform the installation again. But if user's home directory exist and you still see this error then this error can be ignored.

2. Installation being performed as a root user:
If the application is being installed as root user and you see above errors then it should be investigated. Please open a PMR for assistance from IBM.
response:
To fix the problem of the second DE instance causing upgrade failure, you can follow these steps:

1. Make sure the current upgrade is using the same version of JVM as the original install.
2. Remove the newly created DE that shouldn't have been created and create a link to the existing DE. This can be done by:
   - Backing up the original DE installation directory: `<DE_HOME>/.acsi_<FQDN>`
   - Moving the new invalid DE directory to a backup directory: `<DE_HOME>/.acsi_HOSTNAME`
   - Creating a soft link to the "good" DE install:
      ```
      cd <DE_HOME>
      ln -s <DE_HOME>/.acsi_<FQDN> .acsi_HOSTNAME
      ```
   - Confirming the link was created successfully by checking the output of `ls -lart`, it should look like this: `lrwxrwxrwx 1 nc_admin ncoadmin <date> .acsi_HOSTNAME -> <DE_HOME>/.acsi_<FQDN>`
3. Retry the product install after confirming there is no required cleanup before retrying the Product upgrade.
overall_supported_explanation: The response is supported overall by Document 1, which provides detailed steps on how to handle the problem of a second DE installation which caused a product upgrade failure. It talks about using the same JVM version, removing the redundant DE, creating a correct link, and reattempting the installation. These steps are explicitly outlined in Document 1.

response: In Rhapsody Statechart, you can achieve the functionality of an action that must occur during the whole time the state is active by using "Actions on Entry" and "Actions on Exit". The "Actions on Entry" are executed when the state becomes active, and the "Actions on Exit" are executed when the state becomes inactive. By combining these two sets of actions creatively, you can simulate an action that occurs continuously during the active state. You can also consider using additional states or transitions within the statechart to trigger specific actions at different points during the state's activation if needed.

documents:
active; directory; smtp; distinguished; name; AFUP0010E; connector; e-mail; server TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 I am configuring IBM Content Collector for Microsoft Exchange and I need to know the acceptable formats that I can use to specify Active Directory user ID in the Configuration Manager of IBM Content Collector. 

SYMPTOM
A connection to the Active Directory is not established. Task routes are not able to archive messages in either automatic or interactive mode.


CAUSE
Log on credentials will not be accepted by the Active Directory. This is due to the method used by the Active Directory administrator when they set up and configured their Active Directory domain.

ENVIRONMENT
Microsoft Exchange Server 2003 and Microsoft Exchange Server 2008

DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
Look for the following conditions:


 * Automatic or interactive task routes do not archive messages 
 * Error messages appear in the C:\Program Files\IBM\ContentCollector\ctms\Log\afu_mailconnector_trace.log which indicates a failed connection attempt. The message will typically resemble the following:
   SEVERE AFUP0010E Could not create global session. Error message: com.ibm.afu.mailinterface.MailException: javax.naming.AuthenticationException: [LDAP: error code 49 -80090308: LdapErr: DSID-0C090334, comment: AcceptSecurityContext error, data 525, vece ] [com.ibm.afu.mailconnector.MailConnectorService: initMailinterface]

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
When you are setting up the E-Mail Server Connector in the Content Collector configuration manager, configure the User ID field on the Active Directory tab using one of the following three formats: 

 * smtp format: userid@company.com 
 * domain and userid format: domain\userid 
 * distinguished name (DN) format: CN=Username, CN=Users, DC=MyCompany, DC=com


After both the ICC Task Routing Engine and the ICC E-Mail Connector services have been started, check the afu_mailconnector_trace.log to determine if you have obtained a successful connection to the mail server. If you see the "CONFIG ConnectorContext created" message in the C:\Program Files\IBM\ContentCollector\ctms\Log\afu_mailconnector_trace.log, it means you have connected successfully. If each format attempt fails, you should attempt to connect to the Active Directory server by using an application such as LDAPsoft LDAP Browser to validate the user ID format and password. Supply the user ID and password credentials in the proper format when you configure the Content Collector Active Directory connector.

question:
Why can't I connect to HTTPS server using Installation Manager 1.7.2? 

When using IBM Installation Manager 1.7.2 to connect to a HTTPS server, I get the following message:

The following repositories are not connected: https://www.ibm.com/software/repositorymanager/service/com.ibm. websphere.ND.v85/8.5.5.2.

When I try to hit the URL, I get a 404 error.
documents:
IM; rad; TLS protocol; Update Site; Installation Manager TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 When using IBM Installation Manager to search for product updates, latest updates might not be detected and you may see errors related to some repositories under https://www.ibm.com/ [https://www.ibm.com/] not connected. 

CAUSE
The IBM Update Site currently requires the use of the TLS protocol; however, the TLS protocol is not supported by IBM Installation Manager 1.7.3.1 or earlier. 

ENVIRONMENT
Any of the following Rational Integrated Development Environment products can be installed by IBM Installation Manager: 

 * Rational Application Developer for WebSphere Software [http://www.ibm.com/software/products/en/application] 
 * Rational Business Developer [http://www.ibm.com/software/products/en/busdeveloper] 
 * Rational Developer for AIX and Linux AIX COBOL Edition [http://www.ibm.com/software/products/en/dev-aixcobol] 
 * Rational Developer for AIX and Linux C/C++ Edition [http://www.ibm.com/software/products/en/dev-c-cpp] 
 * Rational Developer for System z [http://www.ibm.com/software/products/en/developerforsystemz] 
 * Rational Developer for i [http://www.ibm.com/software/products/en/dev-ibm-i] 
 * Rational Software Architect [http://www.ibm.com/software/products/en/ratisoftarch] 
 * Rational Software Architect for WebSphere Software [http://www-03.ibm.com/software/products/en/swarchitect-websphere]

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
To resolve the issue, you need to update IBM Installation Manager to version 1.8 or later. 


 

WORKAROUND: 

You can work around this issue by adding the Service Repository URLs of the products in the repository list. 

Complete the following steps: 

 1. Start IBM Installation Manager. 
 2. Click File > Preferences. 
 3. In the Preferences window, from the list, click Repositories. 
 4. In the Repositories window, click the Add Repository button. 
 5. In the Add Repository window, add the service repository URL and click OK.


The service repository URL needs to be in this format:
https://www.ibm.com/software/repositorymanager/localService/[OfferingID]/ 


where [OfferingID] is the offering ID of your product. 

For example:
https://www.ibm.com/software/repositorymanager/localService/com.ibm.rational.application.developer.v91


Note: To find the offering ID of all the installed products, you can look at the file names in the following location: 

 * Microsoft Windows: C:\ProgramData\IBM\Installation Manager\installRegistry\metadata\Offerings
   
 * Linux: /var/ibm/InstallationManager/installRegistry/metadata/Offerings


For each of the jar files there, the string before the _ character is the offering ID. For example, for the file com.ibm.cic.agent_1.7.2000.20140227_0303.jar, the offering ID is com.ibm.cic.agent.

RELATED INFORMATION
#Product updates not found using IM 1.7.3.1 or older [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21688067]
